stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides trade barbs over truce as Kremlin says Russia will not extend it unless Kyiv agrees to its terms.

stories.headline: US, Iran Fail to Reach Peace Agreement After Marathon Talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran ended direct talks in Pakistan without an agreement, putting a fragile ceasefire at risk and raising doubts over efforts to end a six-week war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy supplies.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Reopening the critical waterway is a sticking point in negotiations as oil supply bottlenecks prompt a global energy crisis

stories.headline: Trump Says US Will Blockade Hormuz Strait After Iran Talks Fail
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US would blockade the Strait of Hormuz following the collapse of peace talks with Iran in Islamabad. Axios National Security Editor Dave Lawler, Bloomberg News White House Reporter Jeff Mason and Congressional Reporter Alicia Diaz join Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
stories.summary: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.

stories.headline: US and Iran Have Failed to Reach an Agreement, JD Vance Says
stories.summary:
The US and Iran failed to reach an agreement after marathon talks in Pakistan on the weekend, a major setback to finding a lasting resolution to the six-week war in the Middle East.

Vice President JD Vance said negotiators will return to the US without a deal after Iran failed to give a commitment it would not seek a nuclear weapon. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.headline: Hormuz Tracker: Limited Transits Before Trump Announces Blockade
stories.summary: Shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz continued to operate at reduced levels on Sunday, before President Donald Trump announced an immediate blockade of the critical maritime chokepoint by the US Navy.

stories.headline: After 21 Hours of Talks With Iran, Vance Leaves Without a Deal
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance landed in Islamabad on Saturday tasked with ending six weeks of war and overcoming 47 years of enmity — 21 hours later, he flew home without a deal.

stories.headline: Trump says US to blockade ships crossing Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the US Navy will immediately blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Hungarians vote in hard-fought election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years
stories.summary: Rightwing leader trails in polls to Péter Magyar, despite support from JD Vance on recent visitHungarians are heading to the ballot boxes to vote in a landmarkparliamentary election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power and potentially reshape the central European country’s relations with the EU, Moscow and Washington.During the campaign, Orbán – the EU’s longest-serving leader – has trailed in the polls as he faces an unprecedente

stories.headline: US-Iran peace talks underway with Vance, Ghalibaf in Islamabad
Police clear Irish fuel protesters from several locations
stories.summary: A cabinet meeting will be held on Sunday where an update on ongoing fuel crisis talks will be given.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

stories.headline: Benin holds presidential election four months after failed coup
stories.summary: As president Patrice Talon steps down after a decade, the west African country’s finance minister is favourite to winThis Sunday, just four months after a failed coup, Benin heads to the polls for a presidential election that feels more like a coronation than a contest.Patrice Talon, the businessman turned politician who has been president since 2016, is ineligible to run again after serving two five-year terms. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Hungarians vote in record numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
stories.summary: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, but the record turnout for any Hungarian election does not necessarily favour either party.

stories.headline: Earnings Season Kicks Off With War, AI Threat Among Key Worries
stories.summary: Earnings season is about to start at a crucial time for stocks traders whipsawed by war in the Middle East, concerns around private credit and the disruptive threat of artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Oil-Rich Latin America Lures Traders Navigating War Jitters
stories.summary: Latin American assets have emerged as a haven for emerging-market investors trying to navigate an increasingly volatile global backdrop, buoyed by a roster of oil exporters and greater insulation from tensions in the Middle East.

stories.headline: China Unveils Taiwan Goodwill Steps After Opposition Talks
stories.summary: China announced a series of policy measures to demonstrate “goodwill” toward Taiwan on Sunday, following a landmark meeting between President Xi Jinping and Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia Says East-West Pipeline Restored to Full Capacity
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has restored the full pumping capacity of its East-West pipeline to 7 million barrels a day, rehabilitating a vital link for oil exports via the Red Sea.

stories.headline: Irish police clear Dublin blockade staged by fuel price protesters
stories.summary: Hundreds of officers deployed to regain control of O’Connell Street on sixth day of protests by farmers and hauliersPolice have cleared a blockade of central Dublin by farmers and hauliers who were protesting about fuel prices, signalling a possible end to six days of protests that have rocked Ireland.Mounted units and hundreds of officers regained control of O’Connell Street in a peaceful operation that emptied the thoroughfare of trucks and tra

stories.headline: Benin holds presidential election amid deteriorating security situation
stories.summary: Benin is facing harsh economic conditions and security challenges that its new leader will have to address.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia says key oil pipeline back to full capacity after attacks
stories.summary: The Ministry of Energy says the East-West pipeline is back to pumping about 7 million barrels per day.

stories.headline: Malnourished Afghan minors trapped as two wars cut off supplies
Orbán and Magyar vote in high-stakes Hungarian election watched by EU
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán and Péter Magyar vote in a pivotal Hungarian election with record turnout expected, as the result could reshape Budapest’s role in the EU.

stories.headline: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.headline: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.headline: Peru Casting Ballots for President, Bicameral Congress in Bid for Stability
stories.summary: Peruvians are heading to the polls Sunday to elect a new president from among a record 36 candidates and to overhaul the legislature, which for the first time in over three decades will return to a bicameral system.

stories.headline: Training for the Paris Marathon Using ChatGPT
stories.summary: Bloomberg News Senior Editor for US Economics & Government Derek Wallbank used ChatGPT to train for the Paris Marathon. He sits down with Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend before his big race to discuss. Watch the show LIVE every Saturday and Sunday morning. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Norway Averts Strike as Unions Clinch Wage Deal With Employers
stories.summary: Norway’s largest trade unions reached a wage deal with employers for private-sector workers, preventing a strike in western Europe’s top energy exporter.

stories.headline: Nation's Top Banks Set to Release Earnings After Tough Quarter
stories.summary: The KBW Bank Index had its weakest quarterly performance since 2023, which is raising concerns about the banking sector's recent challenges ahead of the upcoming earnings report from Wall Street's six largest banks. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Analyst Herman Chan and Bloomberg News Managing Editor Kristine Aquino provide insight on what investors might expect to Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Event Bets Pose a Problem for Wall Street Firms Looking to Trade
stories.summary: Prediction markets are booming. Wagers on everything from the return of Jesus Christ to who will win the World Cup have driven weekly volumes on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi into the billions of dollars.

stories.headline: India Forex Regulator Criticizes Banks’ Rupee Arbitrage Trades
stories.summary: A senior Reserve Bank of India official criticized foreign-exchange market makers for their role in aggravating the rupee’s weakness during the Middle East tensions, as the regulator keeps up its tough messaging stance in its defense of the currency.

stories.headline: Peru votes for ninth president in less than decade
stories.summary: Voters to choose from 35 presidential candidates, including a comedian, a media baron and a political dynasty heiress.

stories.headline: Voters cast ballots in pivotal Hungarian election
stories.summary: Hungarians could see opposition leader Peter Magyar oust Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has ruled for 16 years

stories.headline: Explaining Benin’s presidential election
stories.summary: Benin is voting for its next president. Al Jazeera’s Ahmed Idris is in Cotonou, explaining what’s at stake.

stories.headline: More than 500 people arrested at London rally for Palestine Action
stories.summary: Protest organisers accuse UK government of complicity in Gaza genocide as arrests made at Trafalgar Square rally.

stories.headline: Japan's SoftBank launches unit to develop homegrown AI
Japanese board game company makes its pitch to the global market
Uniqlo growth propels Fast Retailing to Japan's No. 3 by market cap
Gas price surge kick-starts US demand for used EVs
Japan's independent power retailers halt corporate contracts as costs spike
Trump's latest global tariffs challenged in federal court Friday
Sumitomo Forestry bets big on US housing market, eyeing local majors
EU’s largest economies vs top US states: How do they compare in GDP?
stories.summary: Top EU countries and US states appear one after the other in economic size rankings. However, US states surpass the top EU economies in GDP per capita, both in nominal terms and purchasing power.

stories.headline: Vance, who wasn’t keen on Iran war, now tasked with trying to end it
stories.summary: The weekend’s peace talks in Islamabad mark Vance’s highest-profile assignment as vice president, with concomitant political risk.

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
stories.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

stories.headline: A Frantic Race for Barrels Is Gripping the Global Oil Market
stories.summary: While investors focused on the fragile Iranian ceasefire last week, a desperate scramble for cargoes has been playing out in the oil market, as traders and refiners scour the globe for immediately available supplies.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: Irish cabinet meeting to be held to discuss ongoing fuel protests
stories.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations in the Republic of Ireland have run out of fuel as the protests and blockades continued for a fifth day.

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: The high-stakes diplomacy that led to Pakistan hosting US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Critical minerals, cryptocurrency, and CIA co-operation - how Asim Munir, Trump’s “favourite field marshal”, may have pulled off the diplomatic coup of the century.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
stories.summary: Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South KoreaSevere Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. According to the Australian Bureau of Meteorology, Maila had peak sustained winds of 115mph (185km/h), with gusts up to 160mph on Thursday, making it the strongest cyclone recorded this far north in the Solomon S

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

stories.headline: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.
